channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Harrison Ford gets an honorary doctorate and addresses the class of 2026
item.description: Harrison Ford now has an honorary doctorate from Arizona State University, and his speech was cheered by the ASU Class of 2026.
item.content:encoded: Harrison Ford now has an honorary doctorate from Arizona State University, and his speech was cheered by the ASU Class of 2026.
item.dc:creator: Ayesha Rascoe

item.title: Sunday Puzzle: Between the lines
item.description: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe plays the puzzle with WAMU listener Erin Kealiher and Weekend Edition Puzzlemaster Will Shortz.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe plays the puzzle with WAMU listener Erin Kealiher and Weekend Edition Puzzlemaster Will Shortz.
item.dc:creator: Will Shortz

item.title: Bulgarian banger 'Bangaranga' bags country its 1st Eurovision win
item.description: Bulgaria was not among the favorites to win. But performer Dara's catchy, banging anthem bested 24 other nations at the 70th edition of the international singing extravaganza.
item.content:encoded:
Bulgaria was not among the favorites to win. But performer Dara's catchy, banging anthem bested 24 other nations at the 70th edition of the international singing extravaganza.

(Image credit: Helmut Fohringer)
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman

item.title: 'Wait Wait' for May 16. 2026: With Not My Job guest Ken Jennings
item.description: This week, Wait Wait is live in Chicago with host Peter Sagal, special guest Ken Jennings and panelists Tom Bodett, Joyelle Nicole Johnson, and Faith Salie
item.content:encoded:
This week, Wait Wait is live in Chicago with host Peter Sagal, special guest Ken Jennings and panelists Tom Bodett, Joyelle Nicole Johnson, and Faith Salie

(Image credit: Araya Doheny)

item.title: Library of Congress unveils new children's exhibit with interactive stations
item.description: A new exhibit at the Library of Congress brings children face to face with primary sources, introducing a new generation to the slow-burn thrill of researching history.
item.content:encoded: A new exhibit at the Library of Congress brings children face to face with primary sources, introducing a new generation to the slow-burn thrill of researching history.

item.title: British musical 'Battle of Cable Street' about 1936 anti-fascist uprising debuts in NYC
item.description: Ninty years ago, a mostly Jewish, working class community in London came together to resist a fascist march through their neighborhood. Now, a new musical looks back at the "Battle of Cable Street."
item.content:encoded: Ninty years ago, a mostly Jewish, working class community in London came together to resist a fascist march through their neighborhood. Now, a new musical looks back at the "Battle of Cable Street."
item.dc:creator: Fatima Al-Kassab

item.title: How Tamara Rojo is remaking ballet
item.description: From championing women choreographers to upending classics, the former Royal Ballet star turned artistic director of the San Francisco Ballet doesn't play it safe.
item.content:encoded:
From championing women choreographers to upending classics, the former Royal Ballet star turned artistic director of the San Francisco Ballet doesn't play it safe.

(Image credit: Karolina Kuras)
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman

item.title: The Eurovision Song Contest reaches its grand final with pop and protests
item.description: The final of the Eurovision Contest arrives Saturday, with tight security and rainy weather failing to dent the enthusiasm of fans, or the opposition of critics who think Israel shouldn't be invited.
item.content:encoded:
The final of the Eurovision Contest arrives Saturday, with tight security and rainy weather failing to dent the enthusiasm of fans, or the opposition of critics who think Israel shouldn't be invited.

(Image credit: Martin Meissner)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: 'Pocket gardens' give native plants a place to thrive in cities
item.description: "Pocket gardens" of native plant species are becoming more common in urban areas. We tag along with a volunteer tending to tiny gardens in a neighborhood of Washington, D.C.
item.content:encoded: "Pocket gardens" of native plant species are becoming more common in urban areas. We tag along with a volunteer tending to tiny gardens in a neighborhood of Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Julie Depenbrock

item.title: Actor and musician Rita Wilson talks about what she learned from her parents
item.description: On Wild Card, well-known guests answer the kinds of questions we often think about but don't talk about. Actor and musician Rita Wilson talks about what she learned from her parents.
item.content:encoded: On Wild Card, well-known guests answer the kinds of questions we often think about but don't talk about. Actor and musician Rita Wilson talks about what she learned from her parents.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Martin
